text:
On a moonlit night, two friends decide to embark on a treasure hunt. They’re searching in the forgotten attic of the old Abenby house.

“Hey, didn’t you used to run a bookshop here?” I ask.

“So I did,” she murmurs. “But then I started collecting secrets.”

They rummage through chests and desks. His eyes light up when he spots an old leather-bound tome.

“What’s this?” he asks.

“I used to sell it.”

“I’ll buy it!”

They agree. That night, they decipher cryptic clues hidden under cushions and behind bookends. At dawn, they discover a hidden panel.

A key shines in the sunlight. The door swings open.

They step into an old library, now empty except for a weathered librarian statue. Beside it, a silver platter holds the treasure they’ve won: a map to a pirate’s treasure.

“There!” he says.

As they leave, an owl hoots, echoing their
